content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print all the **leaders** in the array. An element is a leader if it is strictly greater than all the elements to its right side. And the rightmost element is always a leader.


Input Format

The first line contains NNN, the number of elements in an array.
The second line contains NNN integers, denoting the elements in the array.


Output Format
In a single line output all the leaders in the given array.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of elements in the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers denoting the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing all leaders of the given array in the order they appear from left to right, separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Scanning from right, leaders are 2, then 5 (greater than 2), then 17 (greater than 5). Reversing gives 17 5 2.
Only the rightmost element is a leader because all others are not strictly greater than the elements to their right.
The array is strictly increasing, so only the last element 4 is greater than all elements to its right.

solution.approach: Scanning the array from right to left keeps track of the maximum element seen so far. The rightmost element is always a leader. While moving left, an element is a leader if it is strictly greater than the current maximum; in that case we update the maximum and store the element. The collected leaders are in reverse order, so we output them after reversing. This runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(N) extra space for the list of leaders (which can be avoided by printing on the fly, but the list keeps the code simple).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for all test cases combined.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for storing leaders of a single test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        leaders = []
        max_from_right = arr[-1]
        leaders.append(max_from_right)
        for x in reversed(arr[:-1]):
            if x > max_from_right:
                max_from_right = x
                leaders.append(x)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, reversed(leaders))))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
